topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a familiar chime that usually signaled a new assignment, but this time, it was something else entirely. My thumb hovered over the screen, a cold knot tightening in my chest. It was a post from Mr. Harrison, my notoriously strict history teacher. A picture of him, smiling, captioned with a quote about the importance of connection. My breath hitched. I scrolled down, and there, tagged in the photo, was my profile. A small, almost imperceptible tremor ran through my hands as I stared. It wasn't just that he was following me; it was the casual intimacy of it, the digital tether linking our classroom interactions to this public space. I quickly minimized the app, the silence of my dorm room suddenly deafening. I felt a flush creep up my neck, hot and undeniable. I should have blocked him immediately. Instead, I just sat there, staring at the blank wall, the image of his smiling face looping in my mind, feeling a profound sense of awkwardness settle deep in my stomach.

[story 2]

It happened during my late-night study session. I was scrolling through Instagram, mindlessly, when I saw it—a story from Ms. Evans, my calculus instructor. She’d posted a picture of a coffee shop and mentioned how much she enjoyed the creative side of mathematics. Then, I noticed the little ‘Following’ icon next to my name in the corner of her profile. My heart gave a sharp, unpleasant lurch. I clicked over, my fingers clumsy on the glass. There, clearly visible, was a recent update from her—a photo taken near the library where I’d been studying for hours. The realization hit me like a physical blow. I felt a sudden, overwhelming urge to disappear. I quickly closed the app, my jaw clenching so tightly my teeth ached. I spent the next hour rereading my notes, the words blurring before my eyes, unable to focus. Every time I looked up, I imagined her seeing me, judging the very vulnerability of this secret knowledge.

[story 3]

The discovery was accidental, stemming from a shared group chat. We were discussing a difficult concept, and someone forwarded a screenshot. It was a post from Professor Davies, the literature department head. He’d posted a picture of a book he was reading, and in the corner, a small icon indicated that I was in his followers list. A wave of intense self-consciousness washed over me, leaving me feeling strangely hollowed out. I immediately felt a burning sensation behind my eyes. I couldn't shake the feeling that I had stumbled into something deeply inappropriate, a boundary crossed without my knowledge. I tried to rationalize it—maybe it was a glitch, maybe it was a mistake—but the evidence was undeniable. I spent the rest of the evening pacing my room, my movements jerky and restless. The quiet of the night amplified the pounding in my chest, a constant reminder of this unwelcome awareness, making every shadow seem to hold a judgment.
